title: Can't 'buildah push' to dockerhub.com
body:
On a Fedora 26 VM updated to the latest with Buildah installed via `dnf install`, `buildah push` fails as noted below.  I wonder if dockerhub has converted to v2 and that's causing the issue?  This worked a month or so ago fwiw.

```
# cat Dockerfile
FROM docker/whalesay:latest
RUN apt-get -y update && apt-get install -y fortunes
CMD /usr/games/fortune -a | cowsay

# buildah bud -t whale .
STEP 1: FROM docker/whalesay:latest
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:e190868d63f8f8b85b026e53b5724c3c2a4548e1d642953442559cfa5f79b2c9
 62.50 MiB / 62.72 MiB [=======================================================]
{Lots of build info removed for brevity}
Setting up fortunes (1:1.99.1-7) ...
Processing triggers for libc-bin (2.19-0ubuntu6.6) ...
STEP 3: CMD /usr/games/fortune -a | cowsay
STEP 4: COMMIT containers-storage:[overlay@/var/lib/containers/storage+/var/run/containers/storage]docker.io/library/whale:latest

# buildah push whale docker.io/tomsweeneyredhat/blog:whale
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:188ab0d3421d19d043df1ec825d5d9faddf21083a8f3daff73aa438195136f2f
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:079c8ca7aeb2490ddf6ffbcfeef5739d3e94d14ad95b154f99eea2323363846c

Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:4f4fb700ef54461cfa02571ae0db9a0dc1e0cdb5577484a6d75e68dc38e8acc1

Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:6f247a045fefecf66025564554dea04f3cf79ec5d0207d8a98dfacfda2116380

Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:243e1699db27fcbc4e120da1cfc035199b7852e231374c5796b666a4505dbd0f
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:81376b9db5bb656cf0b955c898863a9da97f8a4d3fb75f291f2fde976cd4d2ae
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:4f4fb700ef54461cfa02571ae0db9a0dc1e0cdb5577484a6d75e68dc38e8acc1
Copying blob sha256:b4c5fa97fdc4b1833638526c8b2869b141692741c362625c23629e0189d6d425
 0 B / 24.17 MiB [-------------------------------------------------------------]
ERRO[0030] error pushing image "whale" to "docker://docker.io/tomsweeneyredhat/blog:whale": error copying layers and metadata: Error writing blob: Error initiating layer upload to /v2/tomsweeneyredhat/blog/blobs/uploads/, status 401 

# buildah version
```

comments.body:
```
Error initiating layer upload to /v2/tomsweeneyredhat/blog/blobs/uploads/, status 401 
```

Are you logged in, with write access to the repo? (I didn’t try this myself yet.)
@mtrmac I thought I was logged in, but I wouldn't put it past me to not have been.  I'll double check.

FWIW a
```console
$ ./skopeo copy docker://busybox docker://mtrmac/busybox-test:test7Nov2017
```
with a pre-existing `~/.docker/config.json` works for me.

@mtrmac it was a login issue after all.  However in my little defence, I'd logged in using "docker login".  What I needed to do was "docker login docker.io".  But I thought that was the default if you didn't include a server? 
I'm going to close this due to a somewhat silly user issue.  Thanks for the diagnosis.

> However in my little defence, I'd logged in using "docker login". What I needed to do was "docker login docker.io". But I thought that was the default if you didn't include a server?

That’s the documented behavior in `docker-login(1)` at least.  But on Fedora 26, `docker login` sets up an entry for `registry.fedoraproject.org`, apparently without verifying the password at all.

Is that expected?
We have changed the default in Fedora to go back to docker.io as the first registry, which should fix the confusion.
